comment_text.sn2.29:4.1: This is perhaps the same godling Susīma who was too lazy to follow Sakka's orders (SN 11.2). Another Susīma is a renunciate at SN 12.70.
comment_text.sn2.29:9.1: Legend has it that on the slopes of Mount Meru grows the vast Jambu tree that gives the continent of India her name, “the land of the black plum tree”. The fruits of that tree are as big as elephants, and when they fall, their juice flows forth as a river named Jambu. The dried mud of that river yields gold nuggets whose unparalleled lustre is highly sought-after among the gods. This is that gold (Śiva Purāṇa 17.16–19).
comment_text.sn2.29:15.4: For reading kālaṁ kaṅkhati bhāvito sudanto, see note in Bodhi, Connected Discourses.

root_text.sn2.29:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn2.29:2.1: “Kassa hi nāma, bhante, abālassa aduṭṭhassa amūḷhassa avipallatthacittassa āyasmā sāriputto na rucceyya?
root_text.sn2.29:2.18: Kassa hi nāma, bhante, abālassa aduṭṭhassa amūḷhassa avipallatthacittassa āyasmā sāriputto na rucceyyā”ti?
root_text.sn2.29:3.2: Kassa hi nāma, ānanda, abālassa aduṭṭhassa amūḷhassa avipallatthacittassa sāriputto na rucceyya?
root_text.sn2.29:3.19: Kassa hi nāma, ānanda, abālassa aduṭṭhassa amūḷhassa avipallatthacittassa sāriputto na rucceyyā”ti?
root_text.sn2.29:4.1: Atha kho susimo devaputto āyasmato sāriputtassa vaṇṇe bhaññamāne mahatiyā devaputtaparisāya parivuto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhito kho susimo devaputto bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn2.29:5.2: Kassa hi nāma, bhante, abālassa aduṭṭhassa amūḷhassa avipallatthacittassa āyasmā sāriputto na rucceyya?
root_text.sn2.29:5.4: Mahāpañño, bhante, puthupañño, bhante, hāsapañño, bhante, javanapañño, bhante, tikkhapañño, bhante, nibbedhikapañño, bhante, appiccho, bhante, santuṭṭho, bhante, pavivitto, bhante, asaṁsaṭṭho, bhante, āraddhavīriyo, bhante, vattā, bhante, vacanakkhamo, bhante, codako, bhante, pāpagarahī, bhante, āyasmā sāriputto.
root_text.sn2.29:5.5: Kassa hi nāma, bhante, abālassa aduṭṭhassa amūḷhassa avipallatthacittassa āyasmā sāriputto na rucceyya?
root_text.sn2.29:6.1: Ahampi hi, bhante, yaññadeva devaputtaparisaṁ upasaṅkamiṁ, etadeva bahulaṁ saddaṁ suṇāmi:
root_text.sn2.29:6.3: mahāpañño āyasmā, puthupañño āyasmā, hāsapañño āyasmā, javanapañño āyasmā, tikkhapañño āyasmā, nibbedhikapañño āyasmā, appiccho āyasmā, santuṭṭho āyasmā, pavivitto āyasmā, asaṁsaṭṭho āyasmā, āraddhavīriyo āyasmā, vattā āyasmā, vacanakkhamo āyasmā, codako āyasmā, pāpagarahī āyasmā sāriputto’ti.
root_text.sn2.29:6.4: Kassa hi nāma, bhante, abālassa aduṭṭhassa amūḷhassa avipallatthacittassa āyasmā sāriputto na rucceyyā”ti?
root_text.sn2.29:7.1: Atha kho susimassa devaputtassa devaputtaparisā āyasmato sāriputtassa vaṇṇe bhaññamāne attamanā pamuditā pītisomanassajātā uccāvacā vaṇṇanibhā upadaṁseti.
root_text.sn2.29:8.1: Seyyathāpi nāma maṇi veḷuriyo subho jātimā aṭṭhaṁso suparikammakato paṇḍukambale nikkhitto bhāsate ca tapate ca virocati ca;
root_text.sn2.29:8.2: evamevaṁ susimassa devaputtassa devaputtaparisā āyasmato sāriputtassa vaṇṇe bhaññamāne attamanā pamuditā pītisomanassajātā uccāvacā vaṇṇanibhā upadaṁseti.
root_text.sn2.29:9.1: Seyyathāpi nāma nikkhaṁ jambonadaṁ dakkhakammāraputtaukkāmukhasukusalasampahaṭṭhaṁ paṇḍukambale nikkhittaṁ bhāsate ca tapate ca virocati ca;
root_text.sn2.29:9.2: evamevaṁ susimassa devaputtassa devaputtaparisā āyasmato sāriputtassa vaṇṇe bhaññamāne attamanā pamuditā pītisomanassajātā uccāvacā vaṇṇanibhā upadaṁseti.
root_text.sn2.29:10.1: Seyyathāpi nāma saradasamaye viddhe vigatavalāhake deve rattiyā paccūsasamayaṁ osadhitārakā bhāsate ca tapate ca virocati ca;
root_text.sn2.29:10.2: evamevaṁ susimassa devaputtassa devaputtaparisā āyasmato sāriputtassa vaṇṇe bhaññamāne attamanā pamuditā pītisomanassajātā uccāvacā vaṇṇanibhā upadaṁseti.
root_text.sn2.29:11.1: Seyyathāpi nāma saradasamaye viddhe vigatavalāhake deve ādicco nabhaṁ abbhussakkamāno sabbaṁ ākāsagataṁ tamagataṁ abhivihacca bhāsate ca tapate ca virocati ca;
root_text.sn2.29:11.2: evamevaṁ susimassa devaputtassa devaputtaparisā āyasmato sāriputtassa vaṇṇe bhaññamāne attamanā pamuditā pītisomanassajātā uccāvacā vaṇṇanibhā upadaṁseti.
root_text.sn2.29:12.1: Atha kho susimo devaputto āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ ārabbha bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn2.29:14.1: Atha kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ ārabbha susimaṁ devaputtaṁ gāthāya paccabhāsi:

translation_text.sn2.29:1.2: Then Venerable Ānanda went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him,
translation_text.sn2.29:2.1: “Sir, who on earth would not endorse Venerable Sāriputta unless they’re a fool, a hater, delusional, or mentally deranged?
translation_text.sn2.29:2.18: Who on earth would not endorse Venerable Sāriputta unless they’re a fool, a hater, delusional, or mentally deranged?”
translation_text.sn2.29:3.1: “That’s so true, Ānanda! That’s so true!
translation_text.sn2.29:3.2: Who on earth would not endorse Venerable Sāriputta unless they’re a fool, a hater, delusional, or mentally deranged?”
translation_text.sn2.29:3.3: And the Buddha repeated all of Ānanda’s terms of praise.
translation_text.sn2.29:4.1: While this praise of Sāriputta was being spoken, the godling Susīma approached the Buddha, escorted by a large assembly of godlings. He bowed, stood to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn2.29:5.1: “That’s so true, Blessed One! That’s so true, Holy One!
translation_text.sn2.29:5.2: Who on earth would not endorse Venerable Sāriputta unless they’re a fool, a hater, delusional, or mentally deranged?”
translation_text.sn2.29:5.3: And he too repeated all the terms of praise of Sāriputta, adding,
translation_text.sn2.29:6.1: “For I too, sir, whenever I go to an assembly of godlings, frequently hear the same terms of praise.”
translation_text.sn2.29:7.1: While this praise of Sāriputta was being spoken, the godlings of Susīma’s assembly—uplifted and overjoyed, full of rapture and happiness—displayed a rainbow of bright colors.
translation_text.sn2.29:8.1: Suppose there was a beryl gem that was naturally lustrous, eight-faceted, well-worked. When placed on a cream rug it would shine and glow and radiate.
translation_text.sn2.29:8.2: In the same way, the godlings of Susīma’s assembly displayed a rainbow of bright colors.
translation_text.sn2.29:9.1: Suppose there was a pendant of Black Plum River gold, fashioned by a deft smith, well wrought in the forge. When placed on a cream rug it would shine and glow and radiate.
translation_text.sn2.29:9.2: In the same way, the godlings of Susīma’s assembly displayed a rainbow of bright colors.
translation_text.sn2.29:10.1: Suppose that  in the autumn the heavens were clear and cloudless. At the crack of dawn, the Morning Star shines and glows and radiates.
translation_text.sn2.29:10.2: In the same way, the godlings of Susīma’s assembly displayed a rainbow of bright colors.
translation_text.sn2.29:11.1: Suppose that in the autumn the heavens were clear and cloudless. As the sun is rising to the firmament, having dispelled all the darkness of space, shines and glows and radiates.
translation_text.sn2.29:11.2: In the same way, the godlings of Susīma’s assembly displayed a rainbow of bright colors.
translation_text.sn2.29:12.1: Then the godling Susīma spoke this verse about Venerable Sāriputta in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn2.29:13.4: the seer shines in the Teacher’s praise!”
translation_text.sn2.29:14.1: Then the Buddha replied to Susīma with this verse about Venerable Sāriputta:
translation_text.sn2.29:15.4: developed and well-tamed, he awaits his time.”

variant_text.sn2.29:15.4: kālaṁ kaṅkhati sudanto → kālaṁ kaṅkhati bhatako sudanto (bj); kālaṁ kaṅkhati bhāvito sudanto (sya-all, km); kālaṁ kaṅkhati bhatiko sudanto (pts1ed); kālaṁ kaṅkhati bhāvito (pts2ed)

reference_text.sn2.29:1.1: bj13.122, cck15.86, ms12S1_541, msdiv110, pts-vp-pli2ed1.147, vri23.78
reference_text.sn2.29:3.1: cck15.87, dr15.87, ms12S1_543, pts-vp-pli1ed1.64, pts-vp-pli2ed1.148, sya15.92
